conversations.content: Today we studied the Skyward Aero Engine Supply Chain Interruption Contingency Plan. If a core component supplier is found in breach of contract and unable to deliver on time...
Core parts supply cut off? The production line will stop! What's the first step in the contingency plan?
then the Procurement Center must immediately activate the strategic reserve inventory and suspend settlement payments to that supplier.
Activate reserve inventory to maintain production, suspend payments to apply pressure! Then handle based on the cause of interruption, right?

Then further action is determined based on the situation: if the delay is objective due to force majeure or local capacity bottlenecks, waive the penalty but revoke the supplier's qualification for Supplier of the Year.
Force majeure or capacity bottlenecks? Waive penalty but revoke award qualification — that's reasonable. What if they threaten to cut supply unless prices are raised?

If threatening to cut supply to unilaterally raise contract purchase prices, it is judged as malicious breach of contract — immediately terminate all cooperation and initiate international commercial arbitration for compensation.
Malicious threat to raise prices? Terminate cooperation plus arbitration — well done! The worst is being deliberately acquired by hostile capital to cause a supply cut, right?

If it is verified that the supplier was maliciously acquired by hostile capital to deliberately cut supply to our company, it is determined as unfair competition — form a consortium to launch a counter-acquisition and pursue joint legal liability of executives.

think: Skyward Aero Engine: core supplier breach of contract, supply cut → Procurement Center activates strategic reserve + suspends payments → three outcomes: force majeure/capacity bottleneck = waive penalty + revoke supplier award; threat to raise prices = malicious breach, terminate cooperation + international arbitration; hostile capital acquisition for cut-off = unfair competition, consortium counter-acquisition + executive liability.
memory: Skyward Aero Engine Supply Chain Interruption Contingency Plan: If a core component supplier is found in breach of contract and unable to deliver on time, then the Procurement Center must immediately activate the strategic reserve inventory and suspend settlement payments to that supplier, then further action is determined based on the situation: if the delay is objective due to force majeure or local capacity bottlenecks, waive the penalty but revoke the supplier's qualification for Supplier of the Year; if threatening to cut supply to unilaterally raise contract purchase prices, it is judged as malicious breach of contract — immediately terminate all cooperation and initiate international commercial arbitration for compensation; if it is verified that the supplier was maliciously acquired by hostile capital to deliberately cut supply to our company, it is determined as unfair competition — form a consortium to launch a counter-acquisition and pursue joint legal liability of executives.
